title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers source registration, subscriptions, following, and ownership into a changed shared-source and tenant subscription model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of shared-source and tenant subscription model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Source Registration
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 7150 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Source Registration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Source Registration, keep Feed Go Source Registration connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
